question_text: A device rated at 2 kilowatts (2 kW) transfers more energy per second than a device rated at 500 watts (500 W). Explain why this is true.
correct_answer: A higher power rating means more energy is transferred per second. 2 kW (2000 W) is greater than 500 W, so the 2 kW device transfers more energy in the same amount of time.
explanation: Power measures how quickly energy is transferred. A 2 kW device has a much higher power rating than a 500 W device, so it delivers energy much faster—this is why it transfers more energy per second.
